To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix and $\lambda$ is a scalar variable.

The given matrix is:
$$
A = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 12 & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
 -13 & -7 & 13 & 2 \\
 -3 & -3 & 13 & -14 \\
 -13 & 14 & 3 & -10
\end{array}\right)
$$

The characteristic polynomial is defined as $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$
A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 12-\lambda & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
 -13 & -7-\lambda & 13 & 2 \\
 -3 & -3 & 13-\lambda & -14 \\
 -13 & 14 & 3 & -10-\lambda
\end{array}\right)
$$

Calculating the determinant of a $4 \times 4$ matrix directly can be tedious. Let's try to simplify the matrix using row or column operations before expanding, or look for patterns in the rows/columns.

Let's denote the rows as $R_1, R_2, R_3, R_4$.

$$
$$

$$
$$
R_3: [-3, -3, 13-\lambda, -14]
$$
$$
R_4: [-13, 14, 3, -10-\lambda]
$$
